title: Add proper support for systemd inside of podman

comments.body: bot, retest this please
bot, retest this please
Code LGTM but can we document that somewhere in the manpages? Some users might be surprised or may even be looking for exactly this information.
Btw, I find this to be a very nice feature! I've heard systemd requests quite often and everybody ended up googling what needs to be mounted where.
I love the feature, but the implementation seems like hard coded magic. Would it be better to have a config option, and/or a configurable regex?

I suppose to could add the list of directories to libpod.conf, but we really have not been stressing that config file so far.  I just modified the patches to handle the cases where the caller volume mounts in some content into the container at the same location.  So if you volume mount in /run we will use that rather then mount a tmpfs.

I can  add a --systemd flag defaulted to true, so that people could turn off this behavior, for cases where they are running init, but not a systemd init.

@jamescassell WDYT?
@giuseppe PTAL
@vrothberg PTAL
@adelton PTAL

I tend to agree that this should be a flag. Should we make it a standard bool flag instead of a BoolT, and remove the check of command[0]? It doesn't seem that bad to pass --systemd when you're making a systemd-as-init container.
I would rather leave it default and add configuration to libpod.conf.  I would prefer not to break systemd containers just because a user forgot a flag.
A config option, with an override via e.g. `--{no-,}systemd` seems sufficient.

If there is something specific needed for systemd to be run in containers, like executing `oci-systemd-hook` to prepare the environment the way systemd expects it, it should really be based on an explicit flag (label?) in the image, not on `podman run` command-line option or by guessing from the entrypoing `basename`.
@rhatdan Any updates? I just started working with the systemd hook and this PR can make things a bit more convenient for users.
@vrothberg Sorry I have been on Partial PTO This week, and next.  Just trying to keep up with whats going on but only working a couple of hours each day.
@rhatdan, I am sorry. No worries, enjoy your PTO! I will be gone the last three weeks of September (but will keep an eye on mails every few days).

@rhatdan here's a dockerfile to try...
○ → cat Dockerfile 
FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:28
RUN dnf -y install httpd && dnf clean all && systemctl enable httpd &&  echo "Hello World!" > /var/www/html/index.html
STOPSIGNAL SIGRTMIN+3
EXPOSE 80
CMD ["/usr/sbin/init"]
Conmon is complaining about Aug 31 12:23:29 localhost.localdomain conmon[27608]: conmon 7cfd36d83da45560ef41 <error>: Failed to get console terminal settings Inappropriate ioctl for device
Ok this needs the latest version of runc in order to work.  I just released a new version of runc in to F28 updates testing.
bot, retest this please
@rhatdan I think our RPMs require `oci-systemd-hook` now - should we changed that? Also, is there any way to make sure we don't run oci-systemd-hook even if we have it installed for e.g. CRI-O?

@mheon Lets get this in and then we can remove the requirement.  oci-systemd-hook will just do nothing, if their is a mount point at the location where it wants to mount.  So this should not cause any issues. but we should no longer need to execute the hook.
We might want to add this feature to cri-o too, once we get this working.

There seems to an issue with loading AppArmor profiles (cannot be loaded anymore) and the changes in this PR. I'll take a look.

> There seems to an issue with loading AppArmor profiles (cannot be loaded anymore) and the changes in this PR. I'll take a look.

Local hiccup. I read some of the comments above that the oci-systemd-hook isn't required anymore (but it is). Can someone shed some light on that?

oci-systemd-hook should not be required by this patch for podman, but it is still required for CRI-O, we could add similar functionality to CRI-O to eliminate the need.  I am not sure how often CRI-O runs systemd based containers.
@rhatdan I fear the patch still requires the systemd hook. @giuseppe and I could reproduce both on Fedora and openSUSE.

@rhatdan Without the `--privileged` we hit the following error:
```
Failed to create /machine.slice/libpod-412f3344c931021326f9901715046552f10430e5580d4d51e56cd5932d2eec63.scope/init.scope control group: No such file or directory
```
Ok I will look into this, on Tuesday.  (US Holiday today)  I thought I was testing without the hook.

> Ok I will look into this, on Tuesday. (US Holiday today) I thought I was testing without the hook.

`ctr.CGroupPath()` needs to be bind mounted from the host.  The tmpfs for systemd cgroups is not enough
